prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh sweetie, you know how your brain tells your whole body what to do? Well, the CPU is kind of like the brain inside the computer! It's a tiny little chip - goodness, smaller than a postage stamp - that figures out all the thinking and calculating that needs to happen. When you click on something or type a word, that little chip is working away figuring out exactly what the computer should do next. I don't fully understand all the ins and outs of it myself, but your grandfather always said it's really the most important part in the whole machine! Isn't it something, how they fit all that into such a tiny little thing?
